basic_details.job_title: UPSC Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - 538 Vacancies Across Multiple Ministries | upsconline.nic.in
basic_details.short_information: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released inviting Online Recruitment Applications for 538 posts across more than 30 central government ministries and departments. Posts span disciplines including civil aviation, defence, forensic science, agriculture, mines, law, and education. Applications must be submitted through the UPSC ORA portal at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/.

age_limit.details: Candidates should check eligibility carefully, as each post carries its own qualification and age requirements. The age limits below are the upper limits for the Unreserved (UR) category; relaxations apply as described after the table.

salary_details.level 10: Operations Officer (DGCA), Air Safety Officer (DGCA), Jr. Technical Officer (Naval HQ), Scientist 'B' posts (ZSI, CFSL x4), Asst. Director (IT) ORGI, Research Officer (Map) ORGI, DCIO (Technical) IB, Chemist (IBM), CME Pune Asst. Professor posts

salary_details.details: All pay scales are as per the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix. The level assigned to each post determines the salary band. Key pay levels across these 70 posts are listed below. | The probation period is 2 years for Group B posts including Junior Scientific Officer (NCONF), Assistant Engineer (Civil) NEC, Sr. Scientific Assistants (DGAQA), Botanist (BSI), Scientist 'B' (CFSL), Research Officer (Map) ORGI, Asst. Director Grade-II MSME, and all UT Ladakh posts unless otherwise noted. The probation period is 1 year for most Group A posts. For posts under ORGI, the National Pension System (NPS) is applicable with effect from 01.01.2004, and the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) is applicable with effect from 01.04.2025; candidates eligible for NPS have the option to enrol directly under UPS on joining.

selection_process: UPSC is conducting this recruitment by selection. The notification does not describe specific stages such as written examination or interview. The selection method will be as determined by UPSC for each post individually. No exam dates or result timelines are stated in the source.
how_to_apply: The following steps reflect the application process described in the notification for UPSC's ORA portal.
Visit the UPSC Online Recruitment Application portal at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/.
Register as a new user if you do not already have an account, or log in with your existing credentials.
Select Advertisement No. 06/2026 from the list and choose the relevant post.
Fill in all required personal, educational, and experience details accurately.
Upload your photograph, signature, and required supporting documents.
Submit the application and save the system-generated confirmation receipt with your registration number.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary
content_sections.text: The UPSC is conducting this recruitment by selection method. All 538 posts are permanent central government positions, gazetted and non-ministerial in nature unless noted otherwise.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Breakdown
content_sections.text: This notification covers posts across many departments. The table below lists every post by vacancy number along with category-wise counts. Only categories explicitly stated in the source are included.

UR-19, EWS-8, OBC-5, SC-12, ST-6 + 2 PwBD

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Age Relaxation
content_sections.text: The standard age relaxations applicable to these posts are as follows. SC candidates receive 5 additional years, OBC candidates receive 3 additional years, and all PwBD candidates receive 10 additional years over and above their category relaxation. For certain posts with a base upper limit of 50 years, the PwBD maximum is capped at 56 years - this applies to the Regional Director (NCONF), Professor Sugar Engineering (NSI), Principal (A&N), and Consultant Ophthalmologist (UT Ladakh). UPSC may relax qualifications and experience at its discretion in writing for candidates otherwise well qualified. Where a grade point system is in use and no conversion formula has been notified by the institution, the following equivalences apply as stated in the notification: For UT Ladakh Lecturer and Engineering posts, ST candidates receive a 5% relaxation at Bachelor's and Master's level, making 55% (or equivalent grade) the minimum eligibility. All posts under the Administration of the Union Territory of Ladakh are exclusively open to Domiciles of UT Ladakh . A valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Tehsildar under the Ladakh Civil Services Decentralization and Recruitment Rules, 2025 (published in the Gazette of India on 03.06.2025) is mandatory. Two eligible categories exist: Category A (holders of or eligible for Ladakh Resident Certificate) and Category B (children of LRC holders or those eligible). Reservation is governed by the Jammu and Kashmir Reservation Act, 2004, as extended to UT Ladakh and amended by the Ladakh Reservation (Amendment) Regulation, 2025 (No. 1 of 2025), and further by the Ladakh Reservation (Amendment) Rules, 2025 notified vide S.O. 77 dated 27.06.2025.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: UPSC is conducting this recruitment by selection . The notification does not describe specific stages such as written examination or interview. The selection method will be as determined by UPSC for each post individually. No exam dates or result timelines are stated in the source.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: All pay scales are as per the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix . The level assigned to each post determines the salary band. Key pay levels across these 70 posts are listed below. The probation period is 2 years for Group B posts including Junior Scientific Officer (NCONF), Assistant Engineer (Civil) NEC, Sr. Scientific Assistants (DGAQA), Botanist (BSI), Scientist 'B' (CFSL), Research Officer (Map) ORGI, Asst. Director Grade-II MSME, and all UT Ladakh posts unless otherwise noted. The probation period is 1 year for most Group A posts. For posts under ORGI, the National Pension System (NPS) is applicable with effect from 01.01.2004, and the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) is applicable with effect from 01.04.2025; candidates eligible for NPS have the option to enrol directly under UPS on joining.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026- How to Apply
content_sections.text: The following steps reflect the application process described in the notification for UPSC's ORA portal.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes
content_sections.text: Candidates applying through the UPSC ORA portal can avoid common problems by following these general practices. Photograph and signature files must meet the portal's specified size and dimension requirements; uploading files that are too large or in an unsupported format is among the most frequent causes of incomplete applications. If a payment transaction fails, wait for the system to confirm the failure before attempting to pay again - making duplicate payments can cause complications that require manual resolution. Use an updated, stable browser on a desktop or laptop for the best experience; mobile browsers sometimes fail to render form fields correctly. Save your registration number and password immediately after registering, as the portal requires them for every subsequent login. Do not attempt to submit your application on the final day of the application window, as heavy traffic on the server is common and can cause timeouts or session errors.

faq.question: Q1. What is UPSC Advertisement No. 06/2026?
faq.answer: UPSC Advertisement No. 06/2026 is an official notification issued by the Union Public Service Commission inviting Online Recruitment Applications for 70 permanent central government posts spread across more than 30 ministries and departments, to be filled by the selection method.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are notified under UPSC Advt. 06/2026?
faq.answer: There are 538 vacancies in total under this advertisement. The single largest block is 50 posts for Assistant Director Grade-II (Economic Investigation) under the Office of the Development Commissioner, Ministry of MSME.

faq.question: Q3. Where do I apply for UPSC Advt. No. 06/2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the UPSC ORA portal at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/. No offline or postal applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification for CME Pune Assistant Professor posts?
faq.answer: For engineering discipline Assistant Professor posts at CME Pune, candidates must hold a first-class (or equivalent) B.E./B.Tech and M.E./M.Tech in the relevant specialisation. For science subject posts (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Geology), candidates need at least 55% marks at Master's level in the subject plus a valid NET/CSIR-NET qualification.

faq.question: Q5. Is a Ph.D. compulsory for Associate Professor posts at CME Pune?
faq.answer: Yes. A Ph.D. in the relevant discipline is mandatory for all Associate Professor posts at CME Pune (Pay Level 13A1) along with a minimum of five years' experience in teaching, research, or industry, of which at least two years must be post-Ph.D.

faq.question: Q6. Can candidates from outside Ladakh apply for posts numbered 60 to 70?
faq.answer: No. Posts 60 through 70 are exclusively reserved for Domiciles of the Union Territory of Ladakh, and a valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Tehsildar of UT Ladakh is mandatory. These posts are governed by the Ladakh Reservation Rules 2025.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for the Air Safety Officer post under DGCA?
faq.answer: The Air Safety Officer post carries Pay Level 10 in the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, with a probation period of one year and headquarters at DGCA HQ, New Delhi.

faq.question: Q8. What experience is needed for the Assistant Legal Adviser post under the Directorate of Enforcement?
faq.answer: Candidates with an LLB degree need three years' experience as an advocate at the bar dealing with criminal or fiscal laws. Candidates with an LLM degree need one year of such advocacy experience.

faq.question: Q9. Are there any posts reserved specifically for PwBD candidates with dedicated vacancies?
faq.answer: Yes. Eight posts in this advertisement carry at least one dedicated PwBD-reserved vacancy: Operations Officer and Air Safety Officer (DGCA), Assistant Legal Adviser (Directorate of Enforcement), Assistant Director IT (ORGI), Mineral Officer Intelligence (IBM), Assistant Director Grade-II Economic Investigation (MSME - 2 backlog vacancies), Assistant Public Prosecutor (CBI), and Private Secretary (UT Ladakh).

faq.question: Q10. What is the recruitment method for posts in UPSC Advt. 06/2026?
faq.answer: UPSC is filling all 538 posts by the selection method, as stated in the advertisement. The specific stages of selection are not detailed in the notification; candidates should refer to the official UPSC website for further process details as they are announced.
